steps.title: Need explicit difference
steps.content: The question asks for emphasis on a difference between the eggs of two species. The key contrast is that Gygis alba eggs averaged 4.46 cm, while Gavia stellata eggs averaged 7.22 cm.

steps.title: Prefer contrast language
steps.content: A strong sentence should not merely name the species or say the study compared egg lengths. It should include both measurements and directly signal that the second species' eggs were longer.

steps.title: Eliminate weak comparisons
steps.content:
• A: names both species but gives no measurements.
  • B: gives both average lengths and uses explicit contrast language.
  • C: focuses on the species' taxonomic orders and study inclusion.
  • D: lists both numbers but leaves the difference only implied.
The answer is B.
